attempt: Move the Ticket 25 Collatz affine-lift kernel from an empirical audit into an exact arithmetic micro-proof certificate.
formal_micro_lemma_statement: For an accelerated Collatz valuation word w of length k, total shift s, and affine constant c, any positive integer cycle realizing w must satisfy (2^s-3^k)n=c; hence 2^s>3^k, 2^s-3^k divides c, and the candidate n must replay the exact valuation word.

false_cycle_certificates.label: false_quotient_cycle_1
proof_steps: Compose the exact accelerated odd Collatz branches for word [3, 1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 2, 3, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1].
A cycle with this exact word must satisfy F_w(n)=n.
Thus a positive integer cycle requires positive denominator, divisibility, and exact valuation replay.

false_cycle_certificates.label: false_quotient_cycle_2
proof_steps: Compose the exact accelerated odd Collatz branches for word [2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2].
The composition is F_w(n)=(2187*n+3389)/2^9.
A cycle with this exact word must satisfy F_w(n)=n.
Thus a positive integer cycle requires positive denominator, divisibility, and exact valuation replay.

false_cycle_certificates.label: false_quotient_cycle_3
proof_steps: Compose the exact accelerated odd Collatz branches for word [1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1].
The composition is F_w(n)=(729*n+745)/2^7.
A cycle with this exact word must satisfy F_w(n)=n.
Therefore (2^7-729)*n=745.
Thus a positive integer cycle requires positive denominator, divisibility, and exact valuation replay.

positive_control_certificate.label: known_1_cycle
proof_steps: Compose the exact accelerated odd Collatz branches for word [2].
The composition is F_w(n)=(3*n+1)/2^2.
A cycle with this exact word must satisfy F_w(n)=n.
Therefore (2^2-3)*n=1.
Thus a positive integer cycle requires positive denominator, divisibility, and exact valuation replay.

closed_obligation: False quotient SCCs are not Collatz counterexamples unless they pass the affine fixed-point kernel.
remaining_obligation: The full Collatz conjecture still needs a global well-founded rank or cover for every non-cyclic exact branch and every larger quotient.
claim_boundary: No Collatz proof and no Collatz counterexample found.
